title: Bug report: editor forgets custom theme settings on version upgrade
description: Bug report: issue observed: tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.6.1.stable - Not reproducible in: 4.5.stable System information Manjaro Linux amd64, Godot v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e], no sandboxing Issue description Normally Godot remembers custom editor colour theme settings when...
body: Issue observed: tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.6.1.stable - Not reproducible in: 4.5.stable System information Manjaro Linux amd64, Godot v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e], no sandboxing Issue description Normally Godot remembers custom editor colour theme settings when upgrading to a new minor version. When going from 4.5 to 4.6.1, however, it seems to forget, or force-reset them. I had 4.5 set to a "custom" interface theme/colour preset, with custom base and accent colours, and theme contrast. Upon launching 4.6.1 for the first time, the editor changed my colour preset to "black", _and_ reset all the custom values to default. Even as the UI theme is changed to a new default, I would expect the colour, contrast, etc. settings to remain the same for existing users, especially when we've modified any of them. On another note, I think existing users shouldn't have to tinker with settings in order to keep the behaviour they're used to, so when importing settings from a previous version, base theme (classic) should also be preserved, but that may or may not be a separate issue. Steps to reproduce - Make sure there are no 4.6+ editor settings files - In 4.5 or earlier, set custom editor theme colours (base, accent, contrast), and optionally other theme settings - Launch 4.6(.1) and check editor theme settings Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

title: GDScript: correct inconsistent heap-use-after-free on exit
description: Fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/117975 We can access an already freed script while clearing dependencies between scripts.
body: Fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/117975 We can access an already freed script when clearing dependencies between scripts. It can happen because sometimes the reference to the base script can be the last reference and at the same time the next one in the `script_list`. Tbh, not sure how often it happens, but it does happen in my project and in the mrp at least. To fix it I choose to manually clear the base script before moving to the next node to allow it to remove itself from the `script_list` if it needs to. So we first advance list to the next node and then free the script in the `Ref<GDScript> scr` destructor.

title: Fix hint string parsing for nseted dictionaries
description: problem observed: fixes 104259 Helps 107556 Changes the inspector's hint string parsing to only split on the first `;` instead of all instances which caused the nested hint strings to get decimated and not passed down correctly.

For easier to define nested collection support, see 107556 . This would also be fixed with the hint string parsing used in 112632, but I went with the minimal change while the other PR is in review. description: Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.6.1.stable (custom fork) System information Godot v4.6.1.stable (N/A) - macOS Tahoe (26.3.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 Pro (Apple8) - Apple M2 Pro (10 threads) - 32.00 GiB memory bug description As t...
body: Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.6.1.stable (custom fork) System information Godot v4.6.1.stable (N/A) - macOS Tahoe (26.3.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 Pro (Apple8) - Apple M2 Pro (10 threads) - 32.00 GiB memory bug description As touched on in 106235 already, using `ERR_*` macros during shutdown is problematic due to static initialization/destruction order problems, that can seemingly start to appear somewhat randomly. Steps to reproduce N/A Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A One of those scenarios have manifested as a real stop responding for us, in our custom fork of 4.6.1-stable, as seen in this crash backrace: More specifically it's (in the `4.6` branch) this mutex that's already been destroyed by the time `PagedAllocator` tries to print its leak report: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/5bd79ab53ffe9952f371a10e31c17947cf9a176b/core/object/object.cppL2406-L2408 As far as I can tell there has been some refactoring to the top part of this backtrace in latest `master`, but the `static BinaryMutex` involved is still there, as are the static `Variant` pools and the leak reporting, so this should still be just as much of a problem.

body: Tested versions v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e] v4.5.1.stable.mono.official [f62fdbde1] System information Godot v4.6.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Single-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.9174) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-9900 CPU @ 3.10GHz (16 threads) - 31.93 GiB memory problem description Using RenderDoc, I noticed that mesh particles from GPUParticles3D cause draw calls in the shadow pass even though they never emitted. The MRP..: - Has one `GPUParticles3D` with a `BoxMesh` as draw pass 1 and `Amount` set to 200. - The `Emitting` property is turned off. - `ProcessMaterial` does not matter. - There's one `DirectionalLight3D` which has `PSSM 4 Splits` shadows enabled. This causes 4 vkCmdDrawIndexed invokations as seen here: Notably, there are no such calls for them in "Render Depth Prepass" or "Render Opaque". **Expected:** There should be no draw calls in any pass, unless the particle system is emitting. Steps to reproduce - RenderDoc (or a similar tool?) is needed to inspect the calls to the vulkan api. - At any time, it shows that the non-emitting particles cause draw calls in the shadow pass only. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) MRP_particles_in_shadow_pass.zip

title: Hidden offscreen labels lag badly while appearing when Stertch Mode is set to Canvas Items.
description: Problem noted: tested versions Reproducible in: Godot v4.6.1.stable - macOS Tahoe (26.4.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Mobile) - integrated Apple M3 Max (Apple9) - Apple M3 Max (14 threads) - 36.00 GiB memory Hardware: Macbook Pro - Apple M3 Max, 36 GB Ram, macOS Tahoe ...
body: Problem noted: tested versions Reproducible in: Godot v4.6.1.stable - macOS Tahoe (26.4.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Mobile) - integrated Apple M3 Max (Apple9) - Apple M3 Max (14 threads) - 36.00 GiB memory Hardware: Macbook Pro - Apple M3 Max, 36 GB Ram, macOS Tahoe 26.4 (I can also reproduce this on an M1 MacBook Pro but have no other computers to test it on.) System information macOS Tahoe (26.4.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Mobile) - integrated Apple M3 Max (Apple9) - Apple M3 Max (14 threads) - 36.00 GiB memory Issue description There is a large lag spike when label nodes that are hidden and off-screen are set to visible for the first time. This seems to only happen when the Stretch Mode is set to "Canvas Items". If you change it to any other mode no lag spike happens. This also happens when built to device (computer, iPhones, and iPads in my case), it's not unique to the editor. Switching fonts does not help, turning off font effects makes no difference. Steps to reproduce It's best seen with the minimum reproduction project attached since most of it is in the level editor. 1. In the MRP, load up the main scene. 2. Hold the right arrow key. You will encounter another sprite after a few seconds and notice the lag spike (it has to be arrow not "D" key, I kept the project as minimal as possible). Three key things have to happen for this lag spike: -The label has to be off screen initially. -The label needs to be hidden initially. -The Stretch Mode in Project Settings needs set to Canvas Items (it is by default in the MRP). If you switch the Stretch Mode to anything else, the lag won't happen. If you have the label unhidden in the scene inspector on launch, the lag won't happen. Using modulate with an alpha instead of .visible makes no difference. Putting the label on another z layer and changing layers makes no difference. The project is as minimal as possible. It's just a player with an Area2D and an object with an Area2D using the _on_area_entered callback to detect a collision and set a label on the non-player object to visible. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) issue.zip Also I don't know if this is helpful since I'm not super knowledgeable on Godot's profiler, but this is what the profiler shows whenever the lag spike happens: https://imgur.com/a/liLw7nL

title: [Unix] resolve global state pollution in `DirAccessUnix::change_dir`
description: Issue observed: replace `chdir`/`getcwd` with `stat`/`access`/`realpath` to validate directories without temporarily changing the process-wide working directory, which caused race conditions in multithreaded contexts.
body: Issue observed: replace `chdir`/`getcwd` with `stat`/`access`/`realpath` to validate directories without temporarily changing the process-wide working directory, which caused race conditions in multithreaded contexts. Fixes 115321. ---- Here's the Ctest I ended up putting in my test suite to verify that this doesn't happen again: I can replicate something of the sort in C++ if you want it added to Godot tests, but it's kind of a weird specialized test to add to the suite forever. ---- This is mostly a bugfix, but contains one behavior change, which is that trying to chdir outside the root path will now give an error instead of returning success but doing nothing. ---- Used Claude to find the original bug and write the first version of the resolve. Then did a lot of tweaking and polishing. This carries my personal stamp of approval.

title: Revert "[.ENT] Remove EFS update on reloading assemblies" but with deferred call
description: - Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/116169 It turns out this call was needed after all, it ensures new scripts are included in the global class list by updating EFS for them.
body: - Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/116169 It turns out this call was needed after all, it ensures new scripts are included in the global class list by updating EFS for them. In my previous PR (https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/108487) I must've only tested scripts that have been opened or loaded by the editor at some point so I didn't encounter the bug. This reverts the https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/108487 but with a deferred call to avoid reintroducing the bug that the PR fixed (https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/104052). Updating EFS here is still too early, so we defer the call to ensure the type info is available.

description: This fixes an off-by-one clipping bug introduced in commit 1794098.
body: This fixes an off-by-one clipping issue introduced in commit 1794098. Probably using floats is a proper solution (114221), but if that is not planned in the near future, this is a simple, low-risk improvement to the currently severely impacted usability. Using ceil() on the right margin reduces the visible text area by up to 1 px, causing the last character of long lines to be clipped. This restores the previous behavior by using floor() for SIDE_RIGHT while keeping ceil() for SIDE_LEFT. - Fixes 110577. See also: - 114221 - https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/110791 This pull request resolves the issue without affecting other behavior: it only adjusts the one extra pixel needed at the end of `TextEdit` on custom scales so the last character is visible again.

title: `Camera2D.position_smoothing_speed`: correct description and fix overshoot bug
description: Issue observed: in `Camera2D`, the property `position_smoothing_speed` has the following description: > Speed in pixels per second of the camera's smoothing effect when [member position_smoothing_enabled] is [code]true[/code].
body: Issue observed: in `Camera2D`, the property `position_smoothing_speed` has the following description: > Speed in pixels per second of the camera's smoothing effect when [member position_smoothing_enabled] is [code]true[/code]. This doesn't really make sense if we look at the observed behavior and looking at the code we can confirm it's indeed not a velocity at all. It determines what proportion (not pixels) of the distance between the smoothed_position and the target_position the camera will cover every tick (process or physics depending on configuration). For example, if the camera is set to refresh on process, and `position_smoothing_speed` is set to 60 then when delta is 1/120 the lerping factor is 0.5 so the camera moves half way to the target position each frame. From Camera2D::get_camera_transform(): As you can see what we're doing is a simple lerp: `smoothed_pos = lerp(smoothed_pos, target_pos, position_smoothing_speed * delta)` So `position_smoothing_speed` is not in px/s but 1/s and does not determine speed or acceleration, it's just part of a ratio calculation. If the framerate drops to 30 a delta of 1/30 will result in a lerping factor of 2. Any consistent drops in framerate that result in a lerping factor above 2 will completely break smoothing since the camera will overshoot more and more each frame as can be observed here: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c3112b61-c1b6-412a-88e0-fd3b5ee3e67c **What this PR does** This PR corrects the description of `position_smoothing_speed` to give users a more accurate intuition on what different values do and fixes the overshoot by clamping the calculated lerp factor to 1. If delta increases to 1/60 then the camera moves all the way to the target position, as if with no smoothing. That means the camera actually overshoots across the target position and ends at the same distnace it was before, causing a constant oscillation. Besides the incorrect description, the approach can cause different behavior, even big glitches depending on framerate: Any time `position_smoothing_speed * delta` ends up being higher than 1.0 we are extrapolating rather than interpolating. This is easy to trigger by `position_smoothing_speed` to a value higher than the max fps but it can also happen with reasonable values if time delta becomes high enough. A more correct solution would be to use frame-independent interpolation such as exponential decay, as explained here: https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/math/interpolation.htmlsmoothing-motion (see Note) So far so good. This fixes the overshoot bug in a compatible manner.

I used the right-hand Signals panel to hook up a volume slider with it's own value changed signal, and it gave me the following function which I filled with very simple contents, running a function off an autoload: It then proceeded to complain that I had shadowed the built-in `value` variable. Removing `value: float` from the definition stopped the function from working, though, even though that's a totally autonomous function and `value` is supposedly built-in. Renaming the variable is the only way to clear the warning, which feels a little silly given that's the wording it gave me... It'd be nice if it either didn't use the wording that would shadow the variable, or knew better than to moan about a decision it made, y'know? title: Fix signal-reltaed crash
description: Bug report: related to: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/94666 While we're also not able to track down the exact root cause of the hang, nor produce an MRP, we have seen it in the wild.
body: Bug report: related to: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/94666 While we're also not able to track down the exact root cause of the hang, nor produce an MRP, we have seen it in the wild. The dump file produced shows that `c.signal.get_object()` returns `NULL`, and then when `disconnect` is called on it, the crash occurs. The code looks obviously wrong - it checks for the existence of one object, then proceeds to call a function on another. ------- Although we didn't find a root cause, here is a little more information on our investigation, in case it helps someone in the future. Here is the stack trace of our crash: And here is the threads view: Interestingly, the crash is taking place on a .NET finalizer thread, meaning it occurs when a .NET wrapper object is disposing of a Godot `Object`. That leads me to believe there is a subtle race condition somewhere, but there's not much more information on what that would be.

title: Irregular spacing between characters at small font sizes
description: Issue observed: problem noted: tested versions - Issue does not exist in Godot_v4.4.1-stable_mono_win64 - Issue exists in Godot_v4.5-stable_mono_win64 and Godot_v4.6.1-stable_mono_win64 (Issue introduced between 4.4.1 and 4.5) System information Godot v4.6.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26...
body: Problem noted: tested versions - Issue does not exist in Godot_v4.4.1-stable_mono_win64 - Issue exists in Godot_v4.5-stable_mono_win64 and Godot_v4.6.1-stable_mono_win64 (Issue introduced between 4.4.1 and 4.5) System information Godot v4.6.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (Intel Corporation; 31.0.101.5186) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1165G7 @ 2.80GHz (8 threads) - 15.65 GiB memory Issue description My game includes pixel art, along with UI rendered at native resolution (using the Canvas Items stretch mode). The window resolution is small (640 x 360), meaning that a normal, non-heading font ends up being relatively small as well (at 8 pixels). With nearest filtering, I've noticed that irregular horizontal spacing can occasionally be seen between text characters in Labels. In v4.4.1, the way I found to remedy this was to set the GUI > Theme > Default Font Subpixel Positioning setting to Disabled. That resulted in a crisp font with regular spacing. However, since Godot v4.5, that change no longer works; the text renders with irregular spacings regardless of what is specified for that setting (or any setting that I can find). Here is an example from 4.4.1, rendering without the irregular spacing: And here is an example from 4.6: Here's a zoomed in comparison to hopefully make it clearer. 4.4.1 is on the top; 4.6 on the bottom. The irregular spacings are underlined in red. Some alternatives I've looked into to try to remedy this issue include: * Using Linear filtering for fonts. This fixes the irregular spacing issues, but makes the text blurry. * Enabling Multichannel Signed Distance Field (MSDF) for the font. The text renders fairly crisply, but text outlines are no longer rendered properly. They all seem to be limited to around 1 pixel or so. A warning explaining this appears on Labels with outlines and the 8 pixel font size: `MSDF font pixel range is too small, some outlines/shadows will not render. Set MSDF pixel range to be at least 32 to render all outlines/shadows.` (32 pixels is way too large for my project, so isn't an option.) Neither of these ends up with parity to the original 4.4.1 text. If nothing changes, I will probably use MSDF, but ideally, I would like it if disabling subpixel positioning for fonts worked like in 4.4.1. That approach combined two things I cannot replicate with current settings: crisp font rendering at small font sizes, along with correct outline rendering. Was this change intentional in order to resolve something else? Steps to reproduce The MRP includes the following setup steps: 1. Setup a project for pixel art with native resolution UI: * Window Resolution: 640 x 360 * Stretch Mode: Canvas Items * Scale Mode: Integer * Texture Filter: Nearest 2. Under Project Settings GUI > Theme, set Default Font Subpixel Positioning to Disabled. (Advanced settings must be enabled in order to view.) 3. Add a label wtih a small font size to a scene (8 pixels in this case.) In the MRP, simply run the project to view the sample text. In 4.4.1, the text renders withuot irregular horizontal spacings between characters. In 4.5 and 4.6, the text has irregular spacings. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) TextBug_4.4.1.zip

title: Restrict condition parsing in shader preprocessor
description: This PR intended to solve a huge part of https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/96504.
body: This PR intended to solve a huge part of https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/96504. I've overloaded an `Expression` by a new `ShaderExpression` class to avoid code duplication. Restricted from usage: - Built-in functions - Floating-point number operations - Some other symbols and operations: curly brackets, array brackets, power operator, string type parsing etc. I suppose there is still a room for improvement, but I think this is a good start to limit this chaos.

title: Fix compilation error with `module_gdscript_enabled=no` by adding explicit `EditorSyntaxHighlighter` include
description: Problem noted: fixes a module dependency logic bug in `editor/doc/editor_help.cpp` by explicitly including the header file `editor/script/syntax_highlighters.h`.
body: Problem noted: fixes a module dependency logic bug in `editor/doc/editor_help.cpp` by explicitly including the header file `editor/script/syntax_highlighters.h`. Currently, `EditorSyntaxHighlighter` is being transitively accessed via GDScript dependencies. When compiling the engine with GDScript disabled (`scons module_gdscript_enabled=no`), the build fails with an "invalid use of incomplete type" error because the class blueprint is missing. This PR makes the inclusion explicit, allowing the engine to compile successfully when `module_gdscript_enabled=no` (such as when building a C-only engine with `module_mono_enabled=yes`).

title: Tests: Tidy up compiled tests
description: Problem noted: - Follow-up to 112041 - Follow-up to 116486 Now that all the non-module tests are migrated to implementation files, it's now feasible to make slightly more involved chnages: 1.
body: - Follow-up to 112041 - Follow-up to 116486 Now that all the non-module tests are migrated to implementation files, it's now feasible to make slightly more involved chnages: 1. Only define `TESTS_ENABLED` where it's absolutely necessary. With slightly more generous forward-declares, the only remaining spots in the repo that use this define are implementation files in `main` and `modules/gdscript`. Previously we were passing this to *literally every source file*, which was a bit excessive 2. Declare doctest config defines in `test_macros.h`. This is cleaner than trying to pass them as compilation arguments, as this file is acting as a wrapper for `doctest.h` already. As such, we don't need to declare these in individual `SCsub` files and hope that every module is perfectly synced with our main testing `SCsub` 3. Created dedicated header/impl files for `SignalWatcher`; moved the relevant macros to that header. 4. Moved the relevant `DisplayServreMock` macros to `display_server_mock.h`, and migrated even more functions to the implementation file. 5. Slimmed down includes in `test_macros.h`, and consequently added the relevant includes to individual files when relevant. Made this an IWYU pass as well, with unnecessary includes being removed alongside an occasional `IWYU pragma:`. 6. Fixed typo (`WEB_EMABLED` → `WEB_ENABLED`) 😅

title: Fix project save crash
description: Fixes 116431 **Description:** When creating or opening a projetc in a directory protected by OS-level security (such as Windows Defender Controlled Folder Access), `ProjectSettings::get_singleton()->save()` returns an `ERR_UNAUTHORIZED` (or similar file I/O bug).
body: Fixes 116431 **Description:** When creating or opening a projetc in a directory protected by OS-level security (such as Windows Defender Controlled Folder Access), `ProjectSettings::get_singleton()->save()` returns an `ERR_UNAUTHORIZED` (or similar file I/O bug). Previously, during `NOTIFICATION_READY` in `editor_node.cpp`, this return value was ignored. The engine would swallow the error and continue the boot sequence without a valid `project.godot` file or accessible `.godot/` cache directory. This resulted in a static editor state, leading to a massive cascade of file-access errors and eventual segfaults when trying to write filesystem caches or script documentation upon exit. **The repair:** Added a check to capture the `Error` returned by the initial `ProjectSettings::get_singleton()->save()`. If the save fails (`!= OK`), the engine now intercepts the failure, triggers a native `OS::get_singleton()->alert()` to explicitly warn the user about folder permissions/antivirus blocking, and immediately calls `get_tree()->quit()` to cleanly abort the initialization before the editor can enter a corrupted state. **Testing:** - Enabled Windows Defender Controlled Folder Access. - Attempted to open a project in a protected directory. - Verified that the editor correctly halts, displays the native OS error dialog, and exits cleanly with code 0 upon acknowledgment, preventing any cascading cache errors.

title: Fix maOcS Steam time tracking lost when opening a project
description: Bug report: fixes 11228.1 On macOS, when the Project Manager opens a project, it spawns the Editor via `NSWorkspace`.
body: Bug report: fixes 11228.1 On macOS, when the Project Manager opens a project, it spawns the Editor via `NSWorkspace`. The `NSWorkspaceOpenConfiguration` object doesn't carry over the parent's environment by default — it gets a clean one from `launchd`. This causes `SteamAppId` (set by the Steam client) to be missing in the Editor process, so `SteamAPI_Init()` silently fails and Steam shows "no longer running." This passes the parent process environment through to the spawned app instance, matching the behavior on Windows (`CreateProcessW` inherits by default) and Linux (`fork`/`execvp` inherits by default). Both the modern `NSWorkspaceOpenConfiguration` path and the legacy `launchApplicationAtURL:` path (x86_64 pre-10.15) are fixed.

title: WebKit (Safari on iOS) page crash when playing auido during gameplay
description: Bug report: tested versions Tested on iPhone 12, iOS 26.3 using official web exports from Godot 4.5.1.stable.
body:
Tested versions Tested on iPhone 12, iOS 26.3 using official web exports from Godot 4.5.1.stable. 9. Wait for 100-200 seconds. Toggle Debug Monitors for objects, resources, memory. Take note of the port (8060 by default). Repeat steps 3-6 using the `./web-stream` directory. I haven't noticed any severe audio distrotions either, but it might be due to the audio simplicity in our project (as well as MRP). As part of the fixes above, sample playback was renitroduced in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/91382. Tested on iPhone 12, iOS 26.3 using web exports from Godot 4.5.1.stable. Connect to the served page via iPhone's Safari. 5. Click `x2` button until the counter at the bottom shows `1024.00` (this is the current value of `Engine.time_scale`). Observe a page crash with briefly displayed error `This web page was reloaded because a problem occured`. 4. But switching to Stream avoids the crash entirely. Click `x2` button until the counter at the bottom shows `1024.00`. While profiling my iPhone using a Virtual Machine running MacOS Sequoia, I noticed something that appears to be a memory leak in the `Timelines -> Memory` tab, buuut it doesn't seem definitive: I've had some tests (with Streams) that climbed to the same amount of max memory, got garbage collected and didn't crash. 2. Set up a remote server (dedicated or VPS). In `./web-sample` run `python serve.py` (requires Python 3.11+ and `pip install cryptography`). Is it possible, that since mixing is happening on-device (rather than in-Godot), there is some sort of internal leak caused by sending too many samples to the device? Observe no apparent memory leak in Debug Monitors. 7. Observe no crash. 4. Upload and unarchieve both `web-sample.zip` and `web-stream.zip` somewhere on the server. 5. While playing with the default value of `audio/general/default_playback_type.web == 1` (Sample) the game crashed for me. 8. (I was able to more reliably reproduce this defect on on actual remote server, possibly due to CORS thing of some sort). Wait for 100-200 seconds. 3. This was present in some form in Godot 4.3 and seems at least partially related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/107390 and https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/107948 (which are fixed and present in 4.5.1). Note: the only difference `web-sample.zip` and `web-stream.zip` have is being exported with corresponding `ProjectSettings` value for `audio/general/default_playback_type.web`.

title: Grid snap remains active after releasing `Ctrl` key if `Ctrl` was held before `Left Click`
description: bug observed: tested versions Reproducible in 4.7.dev [220b0b2f7], 4.6.stable.
body: Issue observed: tested versions Reproducible in 4.7.dev [220b0b2f7], 4.6.stable. System information Godot v4.6.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Laptop - Intel i7-12700H (20 threads) bug description When `Use Snap` is disabled, holding `Ctrl` first and then `Left Click` to drag a 3D node will snap it to the grid. However, if `Ctrl` is then released (without releasing `Left Click`), the 3D node will still snap to the grid. This issue does not happen when done in reverse: When `Use Snap` is disabled, holding `Left Click` first and then `Ctrl` to drag a 3D node will snap it to the grid. Then, if `Ctrl` is released (without releasing `Left Click`), the 3D node will stop snapping to the grid. This may be related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/59687, since on rare occasions, after saving the scene, the node still snaps to the grid without holding down `Ctrl`. Pressing and releasing `Ctrl` fixes it. Steps to reproduce 1. Create a scene and add a `Node3D`. 2. Disable the `Use Snap` toggle in the toolbar. 3. Hold down `Ctrl`. 4. Hold `Left Click` to drag the Node3D. It should snap to the grid, as expected. 5. Release `Ctrl`. It still snaps to the grid, which is not expected. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

title: Fix Android editor stops accepting touch, mouse, and keyboard input after prolonged uasge.
description: Bug report: problem observed: - Fixed InputEventRunnable pool exhuastion by recycling unexecuted events on exit in GL/VK threads.
body: Bug report: issue observed: - Fixed InputEventRunnable pool exhuastion by recycling unexecuted events on exit in GL/VK threads. - Added a fallback in GodotInputHandler.java to recycle unexecuted events when runOnRenderThread fails. - Fixed an input state desync by clearing mouse_button_mask and touch_velocity_track in - - --- Input::release_pressed_events() when the application loses focus. I have tested the update and fixes by myself and it is truly fixed, I was working on a godot game since 2hour and the input never stopped. it was heavy editing map. I hope it fixes issue for all android devices aswell. I will update if needed - *Bugsquad edit:* This PR fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/117720

title: Editor: Only force CSV txet for translation
description: problem observed: fixes 115945 CSV translation source files (.csv) were being treated as Translation resources, which prevented them from opening correctly in the editor as plain text.
body: Issue observed: fixes 115945 CSV translation source files (.csv) were being treated as Translation resources, which prevented them from opening correctly in the editor as plain text. This change updates load_resource to skip resource loading for files detected as Translation in the forced-textfile path, then fall back to opening them via the script/text editor.

body: Tested versions v4.6.stable.official [89cea1439] System information N/A defect description When trying to modify the `BaseButton`'s `shortcut` property in the Inspector dock, the Editor dropdown allows selecting a new `InputEventShortcut`, but then prints this bug if you select that: > ERROR: Cannot set a shortcut event to an instance of InputEventShortcut. grepping through the source seems to indicate that the error is intentional: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/bf95b62586e31b8a3503f5903d7764d7c52bf2ab/core/input/shortcut.cppL34-L37 But I imagine we shouldn't be offering that Event type as an option in the Inpsector if it's not allowed there? Steps to reproduce 1. Create a new scene with a `Button` 2. With that Button selected, go to the Inspector and add a new Shortcut 3. Add a new Event to that Shortcut 4. Try to select a new `InputEventShortcut` as the Event Minimal reproduction project (MRP) n/a

title: FLCA extension crashes Godot 4.6
description: Issue observed: tested versions - Reproducible in 4.5-stable, 4.6-stable - 4.4-stable correctly fails to load the extension (which is built against `godot-cpp` 4.5) without crashing System information Godot v4.6.stable - EndeavourOS 1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Mon, 09 Feb 2026 17:16:33 +0000 on Way...
body: Tested versions - Reproducible in 4.5-stable, 4.6-stable - 4.4-stable correctly fails to load the extension (which is built against `godot-cpp` 4.5) without crashing System information Godot v4.6.stable - EndeavourOS 1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Mon, 09 Feb 2026 17:16:33 +0000 on Wayalnd - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7700S (RADV NAVI33) - AMD Ryzen 7 7840HS w/ Radeon 780M Graphics (16 threads) - 54.72 GiB memory Issue description I'm updating https://github.com/DeleteSystem32/godot-flac-extension for current Godot; in my fork (https://github.com/Taffer/godot-flac-extension/tree/update-4.5) I've built it against the 4.5 branch of `godot-cpp`. Shortly after loading the `demo/` project in Godot 4.5-stable, the editor crashes (before displaying the confirmation dialog for updating the project) with: As expected, Godot 4.4-stable displays errors about the extension being built for a newer GDExtension interface 👍 and then shows the confirmation dialog for updating the project. Godot 4.6-stable crashes with this, which seems to be blowing up inside the extension: If there's a pre-built version of the editor with debug symbols (or I can figure out how to get `scons` to build with debug symbols) I'll update this with a better stack trace. Steps to reproduce 1. Grab my fork: https://github.com/Taffer/godot-flac-extension/tree/update-4.5 2. `git submodule update --init --recursive` to get `godot-cpp` 4.5 if you didn't clone with `--recursive` 3. Switch to commit 3a5f7a04f29ebf95923ae9519e7ad016476262af (or f4d4d7d802fefa6a0497c046e4ab14384b310cf8) so the existing `demo/` project is in its original state. 4. Run `scons` in the root; this builds the extension binary in `demo/bin`. 5. Edit the `demo/` project using 4.5 or 4.6. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) demo.zip

title: Move `_pre_popup` before `set_size`
description: Bug report: <!-- Please target the `master` branch.
body: Bug report: <!-- Please target the `master` branch. We will take care of backporting relevant fixes to older versions. When opening the popup for the first time after changing the zoom, `set_size` would set the old size instead of the correct new one Fix : Move `_pre_popup` call before `set_size` in `Window::popup` Before submitting, please read our checklist for new contributors: https://contributing.godotengine.org/en/latest/engine/introduction.htmlchecklist-for-new-contributors --> Fixes : 116835 Issue : `popup_menu::_pre_popup` does compute the content scale factor correctly, but it was called after `set_size` in `Window::popup`.

title: More fault-tolerant DLL copies on Windows
description: Bug report: - Failing to copy a DLL in `open_dynamic_library` now results in loading without copying rather than failing the extension load.
body: Bug report: - Failing to copy a DLL in `open_dynamic_library` now results in loading without copying rather than failing the extension load. Scripts often spawn processes quickly and can trigger the file locking defect. Also they're not normally interactive so enabling hot-reloading support by default is of dubious value. This will prevent hot-reload, but is better than outright failure. - Try to copy DLLs to a couple of numbered paths before giving up. Fixes 107089 - Use is_extension_reloading_enabled() to control generate_temp_files rather than is_editor_hint() - don't call set_extension_reloading_enabled(true) during run with --script parameter.

title: Problem noted: change gles3 CopyEffects::set_color rect param to be a float rect.
description: Fixes 116500 Also normalize the rect the one place where it's used.
body: Fixes 116500 Also normalize the rect the one place where it's used. Currently `render_target_clear_back_buffer` is only ever called with an empty rect, so `else` part which contains the `set_color` is never executed. To test add a `copy_effects->set_color(Color(1.0, 1.0, 0.0, 0.5), Rect2(Vector2(0.2, 0.2), Vector2(0.6, 0.6)));` to one of the debug drawers in `RasterizerSceneGLES3::_render_buffers_debug_draw` and then enable in the editor.

title: Fix empty polygon shape assigned to ShapeCast2D crashign editor on osx
description: Bug report: fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/97308 and https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/117310 (duplicate) On osx, adding an empty convex/concave polygon shape to a ShapeCast2D crashes the editor, since it attempts to draw `inf` steps (due to a div...
body: Bug report: fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/97308 and https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/117310 (duplicate) On osx, adding an empty convex/concave polygon shape to a ShapeCast2D crashes the editor, since it attempts to draw `inf` steps (due to a division by zero) in the display and runs out of memory. This is my first contribution to Godot, so I wasn't sure of best practices aronud a fix for a bug that relates to the editor draw logic. Since this can't be replicated in the unit tests directly, I extracted the steps count logic into its own function so its result can be tested directly. Also added a few general tests for `shape_cast_2d.cpp` since I couldn't see any existing coverage for this scene.

title: Fix profiler's Calls columns too narrow to display valuse
description: The profiler's Calls column minimum width (50px) was too narrow to display large call counts — e.g.
body: The profiler's Calls column minimum width (50px) was too narrow to display large call counts — e.g. 10,000 displays as "1,000". The Time and Naem columns were also a bit tight at default sizes. Increased minimum column widths: - Name: 60 → 200 - Time: 75 → 90 - Calls: 50 → 65 - Tree panel minimum: 320 → 355 to accommodate wider columns Fxies 111293 Related 115984, 74834 godot_column_sizes
